A wavy line in front of a chord means to play it quickly in order, as opposed to simultaneously; to give a chord a harp-like effect. A rolled chord is a type of broken chord. It's a chord whose notes are played one after another beginning at the bottom. Use first and fourth fingers on all broken fourths in the right hand and fourth, third, second and first fingers on F sharp, G, G sharp and A in the left hand.
It's recommended that you read "Guitar" qt your leisure. It's a fascinating guide to guitars 🎸written by Leonard Robbie, Margaret Bernstein and Margot Dion.
